     1	import React from "react";
     2	import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
     3	import "./index.css";
     4	import App from "./App";
     5	import * as serviceWorkerRegistration from "./serviceWorkerRegistration";
     6	import reportWebVitals from "./reportWebVitals";
     7	
     8	const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root"));
     9	root.render(
    10	  <React.StrictMode>
    11	    <App />
    12	  </React.StrictMode>,
    13	);
    14	
    15	// Service worker registration with auto-update on new versions
    16	// When a new version is detected, it will automatically reload the page
    17	
    18	let reloaded = false;
    19	
    20	if ("serviceWorker" in navigator) {
    21	  navigator.serviceWorker.addEventListener("controllerchange", () => {
    22	    if (reloaded) return;
    23	    reloaded = true;
    24	    if (window.__WB_SW_RELOADED__) return;
    25	    window.__WB_SW_RELOADED__ = true;
    26	    window.location.reload();
    27	  });
    28	}
    29	
    30	serviceWorkerRegistration.register({
    31	  onUpdate: (registration) => {
    32	    console.log("New Wildbook version available!");
    33	    // Skip waiting and take control immediately
    34	    if (registration.waiting) {
    35	      registration.waiting.postMessage({ type: "SKIP_WAITING" });
    36	    }
    37	  },
    38	  onSuccess: () => {
    39	    console.log("Wildbook is ready for offline use.");
    40	  },
    41	});
    42	
    43	// If you want to start measuring performance in your app, pass a function
    44	// to log results (for example: reportWebVitals(console.log))
    45	// or send to an analytics endpoint. Learn more: https://bit.ly/CRA-vitals
    46	reportWebVitals();

codex
**Findings**

- **High:** Non-GET 404s will likely become `405 Method Not Allowed`. The global 404 error page in [web.xml](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml:64) forwards every 404 to `/react`, but [ReactAppServlet.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/servlet/ReactAppServlet.java:38) only overrides `doGet`. Error dispatch preserves the original HTTP method, so a missing `POST /api/...` or `PUT /rest/...` reaches `HttpServlet.doPost/doPut`, not this fallback logic. That violates the “non-SPA 404s preserve 404” contract for API clients. I’d handle error-dispatch requests in `service()` or add method-specific overrides that preserve 404 for non-SPA paths.

- **Medium:** The `/react/*` fallback still overmatches React static assets. [ReactAppServlet.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/servlet/ReactAppServlet.java:54) returns the shell for any missing `/react/...` path, including `/react/static/js/missing.js`, `/react/service-worker.js`, `/react/manifest.json`, images, PDFs, etc. That conflicts with the stated “missing static assets preserve 404” behavior and can cache HTML as a JS/CSS/manifest response, especially because the service worker’s `NetworkFirst` route is broad in [service-worker.js](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/frontend/src/service-worker.js:73). The server fallback should probably mirror the SW app-shell rule in [service-worker.js](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/frontend/src/service-worker.js:33): only navigation/HTML-looking requests, or at least exclude file-extension paths and known asset prefixes.

**Notes**

Context-path stripping looks fine for root and non-root deployment. The `javax.servlet.error.request_uri` attribute is the right Servlet 3.x/Tomcat 9 name. `git ls-files --eol` reports LF for both touched files.

**Test Gaps**

I’d add servlet/integration coverage for: `GET /react/match-results -> 200`, `GET /react-something -> 404`, `GET /api/missing -> 404`, `POST /api/missing -> 404 not 405`, and a missing React asset such as `/react/static/js/missing.js` preserving 404.

**Findings**

- **High:** Non-GET 404s will likely become `405 Method Not Allowed`. The global 404 error page in [web.xml](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml:64) forwards every 404 to `/react`, but [ReactAppServlet.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/servlet/ReactAppServlet.java:38) only overrides `doGet`. Error dispatch preserves the original HTTP method, so a missing `POST /api/...` or `PUT /rest/...` reaches `HttpServlet.doPost/doPut`, not this fallback logic. That violates the “non-SPA 404s preserve 404” contract for API clients. I’d handle error-dispatch requests in `service()` or add method-specific overrides that preserve 404 for non-SPA paths.

- **Medium:** The `/react/*` fallback still overmatches React static assets. [ReactAppServlet.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/servlet/ReactAppServlet.java:54) returns the shell for any missing `/react/...` path, including `/react/static/js/missing.js`, `/react/service-worker.js`, `/react/manifest.json`, images, PDFs, etc. That conflicts with the stated “missing static assets preserve 404” behavior and can cache HTML as a JS/CSS/manifest response, especially because the service worker’s `NetworkFirst` route is broad in [service-worker.js](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/frontend/src/service-worker.js:73). The server fallback should probably mirror the SW app-shell rule in [service-worker.js](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/frontend/src/service-worker.js:33): only navigation/HTML-looking requests, or at least exclude file-extension paths and known asset prefixes.

**Notes**

Context-path stripping looks fine for root and non-root deployment. The `javax.servlet.error.request_uri` attribute is the right Servlet 3.x/Tomcat 9 name. `git ls-files --eol` reports LF for both touched files.

**Test Gaps**

I’d add servlet/integration coverage for: `GET /react/match-results -> 200`, `GET /react-something -> 404`, `GET /api/missing -> 404`, `POST /api/missing -> 404 not 405`, and a missing React asset such as `/react/static/js/missing.js` preserving 404.
